metadata.source_title: Digital subscriptions set media companies on a growth path
metadata.author: Ingrid Laane
metadata.description: Driven by the coronavirus crisis, media companies are making a rapid digital transformation. Because of the lockdowns, media consumption has shifted faster to the internet; the number of digital subscribers to newspapers and on-demand TV services has risen sharply and consumers are buying online en masse. The increased internet traffic gives more branches in the TMT sector a chance to get off to a good start when the economy picks up again.
metadata.parent_text:
Download the full report. (Do you not see a download or are you unable to download the report? Then upgrade your browser to the most recent version.)
[Digital subscriptions set media companies on a growth path (11 March 2021). 261 KB](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/media/ABNAMRO-sectorprognoses-TMT-maart-2021_tcm16-102809.pdf)
Shift to online. Consumers have been ‘stuck’ at home for almost a year, but the internet provides a virtual way out. As a result, data traffic has increased more strongly than in previous years, according to figures from the Amsterdam Internet Exchange (AMS-IX). This internet hub processed 35 per cent more data last year than the year before, while in 2019 growth was ‘only’ 17 per cent. The number of digital subscribers to newspapers benefited from this trend. For example, the number of digital subscriptions at Mediahuis, publisher of NRC and De Telegraaf among others, rose by almost 50 per cent last year. Led by new streaming services and demand for home entertainment due to the many hours spent at home, revenue growth in the Dutch market for TV and video services has also accelerated. While revenue from traditional pay TV remained the same, income from over-the-top video services in the third quarter of 2020 rose by 48 per cent compared with the year before. Online shopping increased by almost 36 per cent. As a result, advertising campaigns are also shifting to the internet, where consumers are increasingly easy to reach. Momentum to strengthen digital propositions. Media companies are using the current crisis to implement structural changes: less dependence on advertising income and more propositions that tap into increased digital consumption. For example, DPG Media in Belgium recently set up its own streaming platform, Streamz, to offer a local alternative to foreign streaming services. Media companies are also differentiating more in their subscription offering. For instance, Videoland now offers a cheaper package because it also contains advertising. Media spending is also expected to increase again in 2021, which gives extra scope to strengthen propositions further.

id: Digitale abonnementen zetten mediabedrijven op groei__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0024
text: Media companies have been working on online propositions for years, but due to strong competition from Google and Facebook in the advertising market and the wide availability of free content, it is difficult to make money from this.

metadata.source_title: Digital subscriptions set media companies on a growth path
metadata.author: Ingrid Laane
metadata.description: Driven by the coronavirus crisis, media companies are making a rapid digital transformation. Because of the lockdowns, media consumption has shifted faster to the internet; the number of digital subscribers to newspapers and on-demand TV services has risen sharply and consumers are buying online en masse. The increased internet traffic gives more branches in the TMT sector a chance to get off to a good start when the economy picks up again.
metadata.parent_text: Media companies have been working on online propositions for years, but due to strong competition from Google and Facebook in the advertising market and the wide availability of free content, it is difficult to make money from this.

text: However, the call to curb the market power of these tech giants is growing louder.

Because of the lockdowns, media consumption has shifted faster to the internet; the number of digital subscribers to newspapers and on-demand TV services has risen sharply and consumers are buying online en masse. The increased internet traffic gives more branches in the TMT sector a chance to get off to a good start when the economy picks up again.
metadata.parent_text: Strong growth in the IT industry. Not only in media companies and advertising agencies, but also in other sectors, the coronavirus crisis has accelerated the digital transformation; for example in health care and education. The IT industry is benefiting from this and growth will therefore pick up again in 2021. Because organisations must also invest carefully in IT in 2021 due to economic uncertainty, growth in this industry will for the time being remain below the long-term average. In the long term, the outlook for the IT industry is excellent. The digitalisation of society continues unabated, which means that companies in all sectors will continue to rely on the technology sector. TMT sector to grow in 2021. For the entire TMT sector, the outlook is particularly good for the second half of this year.

id: Digitale abonnementen zetten mediabedrijven op groei__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0044
text: Because a large part of the vulnerable population is then expected to be vaccinated, the restrictive measures will by then have been largely lifted. This will cause media budgets in hard-hit sectors such as travel and events to grow substantially again, business investment to increase and consumer confidence to rise.
